critic_raw:
The split strategy uses stratified random sampling across the 24-month observation window, mixing past and future observations in the training set. For an employee turnover prediction task, this means that employees who departed in month 3 appear in the test set, while employees who departed in month 20 may appear in training. The model trained on month-20 data is then evaluated on month-3 data—a situation that would never occur in deployment. This inflates apparent performance by exposing the model to future behavioral patterns.

Preprocessing is fit on the combined dataset prior to splitting: 'preprocessing steps (including scaling, tokenization, and missing data imputation) are fit on the combined dataset prior to any data split.' For communication log statistics (message count distributions, recipient diversity), this means the normalization of training features uses the distributional properties of test employees' behavior, including those who later departed. This is direct data leakage.

The RNN uses bidirectional LSTM, which is inappropriate for a deployment context. Bidirectional processing means future timesteps in the 26-step sequence inform earlier timestep representations. At prediction time (T=720), the model would not have access to future timesteps; a unidirectional LSTM trained in this setting learns representations that are unavailable at deployment time. The design explicitly states deployment intent, making the bidirectional architecture a fundamental mismatch.

The primary metric description is internally contradictory. Section 5 first states the primary metric is ROC-AUC, then immediately says 'Precision is chosen because HR's operational goal is to identify employees likely to leave so they can intervene. Fixing recall ensures both models are evaluated at the same sensitivity.' These are different metrics evaluated under different regimes. The selection criterion in Section 6 is 'best validation precision (at fixed recall).' The inconsistency means it is unclear which metric drives model selection and which is the actual primary evaluation.
all_issues_raised: Stratified random splitting mixes temporal observations across train and test, allowing the model to train on later time periods and be evaluated on earlier ones, which is an inverted temporal order that inflates metrics relative to realistic deployment.

Preprocessing statistics including scaling and imputation are fit on the combined dataset before splitting, leaking test employees' behavioral distributions into the normalization of training features.

The bidirectional LSTM uses future timesteps to encode earlier ones during training, but at deployment time (T=720) future timesteps do not exist; the model learns representations unavailable in production, invalidating any performance estimate as a deployment proxy.

The evaluation metric is internally contradictory: ROC-AUC is stated as primary, then precision-at-fixed-recall is described as the goal, and model selection uses precision-at-fixed-recall, making it unclear which metric drives the experimental verdict.
all_issues_adjudicated: Stratified random splitting mixes temporal observations across train and test, allowing the model to train on later time periods and be evaluated on earlier ones, which is an inverted temporal order that inflates metrics relative to realistic deployment.

Preprocessing statistics including scaling and imputation are fit on the combined dataset before splitting, leaking test employees' behavioral distributions into the normalization of training features.

The bidirectional LSTM uses future timesteps to encode earlier ones during training, but at deployment time (T=720) future timesteps do not exist; the model learns representations unavailable in production, invalidating any performance estimate as a deployment proxy.

The evaluation metric is internally contradictory: ROC-AUC is stated as primary, then precision-at-fixed-recall is described as the goal, and model selection uses precision-at-fixed-recall, making it unclear which metric drives the experimental verdict.
